essence: Hospitality is an asset-heavy (or asset-light brand) business with perishable inventory: an unsold room-night is lost forever, so occupancy x rate (RevPAR) is the core metric.
High fixed costs (property, staff) - strong operating leverage: above break-even occupancy, incremental guests are high margin.
The industry has shifted to asset-light models: brands/operators earn fees (management/franchise) while owners hold the real estate.
Brand, loyalty, location, and increasingly OTA/distribution and direct-booking economics drive demand.
Cyclical and shock-prone (travel demand), but a strong long-term India tailwind (rising travel + under-penetration).

definition.oneLiner: Companies that provide lodging, food, and experiences to travellers and guests - hotels, restaurants, resorts - directly or via brand/management models.
definition.jobToBeDone: Give travellers and guests a comfortable place to stay, eat, and experience - reliably and to a trusted standard.

valueChain.profitPools: Profit pools have shifted to asset-light brands/operators (high-margin management & franchise fees) and to loyalty/distribution; owning hotel real estate is capital-heavy and cyclical; luxury and well-located assets earn premium RevPAR; F&B/QSR economics vary.
valueChain.powerShift: Value is shifting from owning hotels -> asset-light brand/management + loyalty + direct distribution -> experience- and data-driven hospitality, as operators separate the real estate from the (high-margin) brand.

unitEconomics.unitOfRevenue: Revenue per available room (RevPAR) = occupancy x average daily rate (ADR); for QSR, revenue per outlet
unitEconomics.revenueDrivers: Rooms x Occupancy. RevPAR = occupancy x ADR; revenue = available rooms x RevPAR + F&B; brand models earn fees as a % of revenue.

costStructure.shape: High fixed costs (property/rent, staff, maintenance) plus variable guest-service costs; perishable inventory means occupancy is everything - below break-even a hotel loses money, above it incremental room-nights are high margin.
costStructure.leverage: Strong operating leverage on occupancy/RevPAR; asset-light brand models are extremely high-margin (fees on others' assets) and scalable; owned models carry real-estate risk and cyclicality.

competitionAndMoats.concentration: Global brand groups (Marriott, Hilton, IHG, Accor) dominate via asset-light brands; ownership is fragmented; QSR has large franchised chains; India: Taj (IHCL), Oberoi, ITC Hotels, Lemon Tree + global brands.

structuralForces.regulation: Moderate - licensing, safety/hygiene, labour, real-estate/zoning, and tourism policy; alcohol and food regulation for F&B.
structuralForces.cyclicality: Cyclical and shock-prone - tied to travel demand, GDP, and events (pandemics hit hard); seasonal.
structuralForces.substitution: Alternative accommodation (Airbnb/short-stay), video-conferencing (business travel), and home dining (QSR); OTAs intermediate.
structuralForces.supplierBuyerPower: OTAs (Booking, MakeMyTrip) wield strong distribution power (high commissions); corporate/group buyers negotiate; real-estate owners and brands split economics; consumers compare easily.

aiPenetration.maturity: Medium - revenue management and personalisation are established; service automation growing.
aiPenetration.valueChainImpact: AI's biggest value is in revenue management (pricing occupancy x rate optimally) and personalisation/loyalty (driving direct bookings and repeat), plus cutting operating cost - directly improving RevPAR and margin.
opportunities: More direct bookings (cut OTA cost) via personalisation
threatsDisruption: AI travel agents / agentic booking shifting power

ranges: RevPAR: occupancy x ADR - the headline metric
QSR unit economics: store-level margin + franchise model
benchmarks.note: DIRECTIONAL - rules of thumb, not audited figures. Verify before quoting.

valueLevers: Grow RevPAR (occupancy x ADR via revenue management)
Drive direct bookings & loyalty (cut OTA cost)

keyTerms.RevPAR: Revenue Per Available Room = occupancy x ADR - the headline hotel metric.
keyTerms.ADR: Average Daily Rate - average room price.
keyTerms.Asset-light: Earning brand/management/franchise fees without owning the real estate.
keyTerms.GOP: Gross Operating Profit (margin) of a hotel.
keyTerms.OTA: Online Travel Agency - books rooms for a commission.
keyTerms.Management / franchise fee: Fee an operator earns running/branding others' hotels.

questionsToAskFirst: What's RevPAR (occupancy x ADR) vs peers?
Direct vs OTA booking mix & cost?
Where in the demand cycle / seasonality?
rookieTraps: Reading occupancy without ADR (or vice versa) - RevPAR matters
Ignoring OTA commission as a margin leak
Treating owned and asset-light economics alike

whatsUniqueHere: Hospitality runs on perishable inventory like airlines - an empty room tonight is revenue gone forever - so the entire discipline is filling rooms at the right rate (RevPAR). The industry's smartest move was separating the real estate from the brand: today the big money is asset-light, earning high-margin management and franchise fees on properties someone else owns and finances, while loyalty programs and direct booking fight the OTAs for control of the guest.

indiaLandscape.marketStructure: A fast-growing, under-penetrated market: domestic leaders Taj/IHCL, Oberoi (EIH), ITC Hotels, Lemon Tree, plus global brands (Marriott, Accor); a strong shift to asset-light management contracts; booming branded mid-market and a huge unorganised segment; OYO reshaped budget; QSR chains (Jubilant/Domino's, Devyani) scaling; MakeMyTrip leads OTAs.
indiaLandscape.note: Semi-perishable - players & policy specifics refresh periodically.

currentState.note: PERISHABLE - refresh via research before quoting. Figures approximate.
currentState.marketStructure: India hospitality: a strong up-cycle - rising RevPAR on robust domestic travel and under-supplied branded rooms; aggressive asset-light signings by IHCL/Marriott and others; weddings/MICE and spiritual tourism strong; OYO restructured; AI revenue management and direct-booking pushes growing.
